name: Scout APM Application Performance Monitoring API
what_it_does: Scout APM REST API for developer-focused application performance monitoring platform. Enables AI agents to manage application performance metric retrieval for response time and throughput analysis, handle slow transaction and N+1 query detection data access, access memory bloat and allocation profiling data for performance optimization, retrieve error rate tracking and transaction error correlation, manage deployment tracking for performance regression detection, handle alert configuration for performance threshold monitoring, access tracing data for request flow and bottleneck analysis, retrieve database query performance and slow query identification, manage application insight recommendations for developer workflow, and integrate APM data with deployment pipelines, alerting, and development workflow platforms.
use_cases: Monitoring slow transactions from performance engineering agents
Detecting N+1 queries from database optimization agents
Correlating deployments with performance regressions from DevOps agents
Alerting on response time degradation from SRE agents
not_for: Infrastructure monitoring without application-layer transaction and query focus
Log aggregation without structured performance tracing context
Security monitoring without application performance context
best_when: A software engineering team using Scout APM wants AI agents to automate performance regression detection, N+1 query surfacing, deployment correlation, and alert routing to development and incident management tools.
avoid_when: DATA SENSITIVITY: APM traces contain full request flow including parameters and query values that may expose PII — configure parameter filtering before automated processing. Performance data can reveal application architecture; restrict API access to authorized teams. Automated performance alerting without baseline calibration generates excessive noise.

auth.notes: Scout APM uses API key authentication. Per-account and per-application API keys. Open-source APM agents on GitHub (scoutapp org). REST API documentation at scoutapm.com/docs/api. No native webhooks — polling-based data retrieval. Developer-friendly pricing and setup. Heroku add-on available. US data storage. Small team (developer-focused, bootstrapped).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan for single app (limited retention); paid plans for production monitoring
pricing.paid_tiers: Developer ($19/mo), Business ($79/mo), Scale ($149/mo+); annual discount available
pricing.notes: Denver, Colorado. Founded 2013. Private bootstrapped company. Developer-focused APM market. Known for simplicity and ease of setup vs. Datadog and New Relic. Ruby and Python strong suits. Open-source APM agents. Heroku ecosystem strong adoption. Competes with Datadog and New Relic at lower price point for SMB and startup market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented in API reference

known_agent_gotchas: DATA SENSITIVITY: APM traces may include query parameters and request bodies with PII — configure parameter scrubbing in Scout agent before automated data access
No webhooks — implement polling for performance alerts; Scout has built-in email alerting but no webhook API; use polling for integration with incident management
Per-application API key — each monitored application has its own API key; cross-application queries require multiple API keys
Small team reliability — Scout is a bootstrapped small company; verify SLA and support SLA before building critical monitoring dependencies
Retention limits — performance data retention varies by plan; verify history depth before building trend analysis or capacity planning workflows
N+1 detection latency — N+1 query detection is async; newly detected patterns appear with latency; don't expect real-time N+1 surfacing
agent_readiness.security_notes: Application performance monitoring. SOC2, GDPR. API key. US. Transaction traces and performance data.

security_score_components.security_notes: APM traces. SOC2, GDPR. API key. US. Application transaction and query performance data.
